description_long: This is a round, bulbous vase with a wide, flared mouth and a single, sturdy handle attached to one side. The body is decorated with horizontal bands and geometric motifs in muted earth tones, including brown, gray, and off-white. The surface appears matte with subtle textural variations, suggesting it is made from fired ceramic or clay. The handle is thick and functional, seamlessly integrated into the body for easy gripping. The interior is visible from the top, showing a dark, unglazed finish. The overall design is reminiscent of traditional pottery, suitable for both decorative and practical use in urban environments.
description: A round, earth-toned ceramic vase with a wide mouth, geometric patterns, and a single sturdy handle. The surface is matte and subtly textured.
description_view_0: Front view showing the handle centered and the wide, flared mouth with geometric patterns visible on the body.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the full profile of the handle and the curvature of the vase, with decorative bands clearly visible.
description_view_2: Rear view with the handle hidden, emphasizing the symmetrical roundness and uninterrupted pattern of the vase.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the handle from a different angle and the continuity of the geometric motifs.
